slides.title: Clothing, Fashion, and Sustainability
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 3: Clothing, Fashion, and Sustainability. Welcome to Unit 3. This unit explores the world of clothing, fashion, and sustainability. You will learn about how clothes are made, why the choices we make as consumers matter, and what people around the world are doing to protect the environment through sustainable fashion. You will also build your skills in multi-word verbs (phrasal verbs like "put on", "dress up" and "turn off") and learn how to use the passive voice to describe processes and actions. Alongside the topic work, you will read the story "A Voice in the Valley", about a quiet girl named Hannah in rural Ethiopia who discovers that her writing can inspire an entire community. By the end of the unit, you will write an eco-project report, give a short speech about sustainable fashion, and... In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use vocabulary related to clothing, materials, fast fashion, and the environment
Understand and use multi-word verbs (phrasal verbs) such as put on, take off, dress up, throw away, and give away
Form sentences in the present passive ("Cotton is grown in many countries") and past passive ("The dress was made by hand")
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use vocabulary related to clothing, materials, fast fashion, and the environment. you will also understand and use multi-word verbs (phrasal verbs) such as put on, take off, dress up, throw away, and give away. you will also form sentences in the present passive ("cotton is grown in many countries") and past passive ("the dress was made by hand"). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: alliteration, rhyme, rhythm, repetition, punctuation, figurative. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Clothing, Fashion, and Sustainability: source text 1
Clothing, Fashion, and Sustainability: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Clothing, Fashion, and Sustainability: source text 1 and Clothing, Fashion, and Sustainability: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Multi-Word Verbs
bullets: What the rule means and why it matters Multi-word verbs (also called phrasal verbs) are special combinations of a main verb and one or more particles...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Multi-Word Verbs. What the rule means and why it matters Multi-word verbs (also called phrasal verbs) are special combinations of a main verb and one or more particles (small words like on, off, up, down, away, out). Together, these words create a new meaning that is often different from the meaning of the verb alone. For example, "put" means to place something, but "put on" means to wear something. Multi-word verbs are extremely common in everyday English, and knowing them will help you communicate more... Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
